summary: Ukrainian forces struck multiple fuel depots in occupied Crimea and Russian‑held Belgorod region, while Russian drones hit civilian targets in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Sumy. Drone activity was reported over the Black Sea, the Odesa Bay and several northern oblasts. Political tensions rose with accusations of corruption in Ukraine’s Supreme Court and diplomatic disputes involving Poland and Moldova.
key_points: Ukrainian units destroyed the "Semikolo­dezyanska" fuel depot near Lenine in occupied Crimea, igniting the site (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian UAVs hit a Ukrposhta warehouse in Kharkiv, partially destroying the hub but leaving people unharmed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30752).
A drone attack on Zaporizhzhia’s residential district killed two people and injured fifteen (Source: @UkraineNow/81778).
Russian drones targeted civilian vehicles in Sumy region, hospitalising four people and seriously injuring a 51‑year‑old man (Source: @eRadarrua/95964).
Two Shahed‑type loitering munitions were observed over Barishivka heading toward Kyiv/Boryspil (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67525).
BPLA activity was reported from the Black Sea towards the Odesa Bay and over Sumy near Putivl (Source: @kpszsu/64504).
Moldova reported a drone crash on its territory, attributing responsibility to Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81769).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces hit fuel depots in occupied Crimea and Belgorod, while Russian drones struck civilian infrastructure in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Sumy, causing casualties. Multiple UAV sightings were reported over the Black Sea, Odesa Bay and northern oblasts, indicating heightened aerial activity. Political disputes involving Ukraine’s judiciary and Polish‑Ukrainian relations added diplomatic tension.

points: Ukrainian units attacked the "Semikolo­dezyanska" fuel depot near the settlement of Lenine in occupied Crimea, causing a fire at a storage site used for Russian military fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
A Ukrainian drone destroyed a Ukrposhta warehouse in Kharkiv, partially damaging the hub but leaving personnel unharmed, according to the company's director (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30752).

Ukrainian forces reported hitting a fuel depot in the Feodosia area of Crimea and a FSB command point in Volokonovka, Belgorod region, with additional drone strikes on the "Ust‑Labinsk" oil depot in Krasnodar (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Ukraine announced receipt of the seventh tranche of €2.8 billion from the EU Ukraine Facility, supporting ongoing defense and reconstruction efforts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30763).
Moldova will co‑produce interceptor drones with Ukraine after reporting more than 20 airspace violations by Russian UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30764).
Russian UAVs struck a residential district in Zaporizhzhia, killing two civilians and wounding fifteen during a night‑time attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81778).

Russian drones targeted civilian vehicles in Sumy region, hospitalising four people and seriously injuring a 51‑year‑old man; additional attacks injured a 56‑year‑old man and a 41‑year‑old woman (Source: @eRadarrua/95964).
A Russian‑sponsored plot to assassinate a GUR officer involved recruiting a 38‑year‑old individual with a $100 k payment and FPV‑drone expertise (Source: @UkraineNow/81764).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on north‑eastern and eastern directions, threatening front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64493).
Russian authorities claimed a drone that crashed in Moldova was Ukrainian, while Moldova blamed Russia for the incursion (Source: @UkraineNow/81769).
Ukrainian forces struck the "Semikolo­dezyanska" fuel depot near Lenine in occupied Crimea, a strategic logistics target for Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian UAVs hit a Ukrposhta hub in Kharkiv, causing partial destruction but no casualties among staff (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30752).
Drone attacks on residential buildings in Konyatop, Sumy region, prompted a rescue operation in a five‑storey building with three injured (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
Ukrainian BPLA sightings over the Black Sea heading toward the Odesa Bay suggest continued pressure on Russian maritime logistics (Source: @kpszsu/64487).
Russian tactical aviation was noted over north‑eastern and eastern axes, raising the risk of air strikes on front‑line settlements (Source: @kpszsu/64493).

Multiple BPLA reports over northern oblasts: near Ripok in Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/95944), along the Belarus border moving westward (Source: @kpszsu/64510), and over Zaporizhzhia from the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/64507).
Two Shahed loitering munitions were observed over Barishivka heading toward Kyiv/Boryspil, indicating possible targeting of the capital region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67525).
Ukrainian radar detected a ballistic missile trajectory toward Odesa Bay, suggesting a long‑range strike capability from the south (Source: @kpszsu/64506).
Russian UAV activity was reported near the village of Dubrovytsia in Rivne region and in Volyn (Source: @eRadarrua/95951).
A drone incident in Moldova was attributed to Russia, highlighting cross‑border UAV threats to northern neighboring states (Source: @UkraineNow/81769).
Ukrainian strikes hit fuel depots in the Krasnodar region, including the "Ust‑Labinsk" oil depot, causing fires (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian UAVs attacked civilian infrastructure in Odesa, damaging a public transport stop and causing two injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81759).
A ballistic missile was reported heading toward Odesa Bay from the south, indicating potential long‑range targeting of port facilities (Source: @kpszsu/64506).
Ukrainian forces reported damaging a fuel depot in Feodosia, Crimea, further degrading Russian logistical capacity in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian tactical aviation threats were noted for north‑eastern and eastern directions, affecting southern front logistics corridors (Source: @kpszsu/64493).
Ukrainian forces used drones to strike fuel depots in occupied Crimea and Belgorod, demonstrating growing UAV strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian UAVs conducted attacks on civilian targets in Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia and Sumy, causing casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30752; @UkraineNow/81778; @eRadarrua/95964).
Two Shahed loitering munitions were observed over Barishivka heading toward Kyiv, indicating continued use of Iranian‑supplied munitions against the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67525).
BPLA activity was reported from the Black Sea toward Odesa Bay and over northern oblasts, suggesting coordinated aerial surveillance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/64487; @kpszsu/64510).
A ballistic missile trajectory toward Odesa Bay was noted, highlighting the presence of long‑range air‑to‑ground threats in the southern theater (Source: @kpszsu/64506).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces destroyed the "Semikolo­dezyanska" fuel depot near Lenine in occupied Crimea, igniting the site and disrupting Russian fuel supplies (Source: @UkraineNow/81765).
Russian drones hit a Ukrposhta warehouse in Kharkiv, partially destroying the hub while personnel remained unharmed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30752).
A drone attack on Zaporizhzhia’s residential district killed two civilians and injured fifteen, marking a lethal strike on a major Ukrainian city (Source: @UkraineNow/81778).
Russian UAVs targeted civilian vehicles in Sumy region, hospitalising four people and seriously injuring a 51‑year‑old man (Source: @eRadarrua/95964).
Two Shahed loitering munitions were observed over Barishivka heading toward Kyiv/Boryspil, indicating potential threats to the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67525).
BPLA activity was reported from the Black Sea toward Odesa Bay and over northern oblasts, showing expanded UAV operational reach (Source: @kpszsu/64487).
Moldova reported a drone crash on its territory and attributed responsibility to Russia, highlighting cross‑border UAV threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81769).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and missile activity continues to target civilian infrastructure across multiple fronts, with notable strikes in Zaporizhzhia, Sumy and Kharkiv causing casualties. Ukrainian drone operations have successfully hit logistical hubs in occupied Crimea and Belgorod, degrading Russian supply lines. The presence of Shahed loitering munitions near Kyiv underscores a persistent threat to the capital, while cross‑border incidents in Moldova reveal an expanding geographical scope of aerial threats.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is leveraging EU funding and expanding drone production partnerships, such as with Moldova, to bolster its air defense and interceptor capabilities. International partners, including Poland, have expressed diplomatic concern over Ukrainian statements and actions, while Russia maintains pressure through tactical aviation and UAV deployments. Regional actors like Moldova are aligning with Ukraine on drone technology to counter Russian incursions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of BPLA movements over the Black Sea and northern borders is critical for early warning of potential strikes on port facilities and the capital. Continued assessment of fuel depot vulnerabilities in occupied territories can inform future Ukrainian targeting cycles. The reported Russian plot against a GUR officer indicates ongoing intelligence‑level sabotage attempts that require heightened counter‑intelligence measures.
